revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= [[File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>

(2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17230799]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= [[File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17230799]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= [[File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17230799]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>

The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

(2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>

Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣|1=tamagaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3078312}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>

'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=|1=Kawashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766691}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>

== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17230799]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=|1=Kawashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766691}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣|1=tamagaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3078312}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17230799]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Wakamiya Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image= File:Wakamiya-jinja.jpg |location= [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]] Takihara 872 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 21 | latitude_sec = 58.6 |longitude_deg = 136 | longitude_min = 25 | longitude_sec = 35 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Wakamiya no Kami |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]) |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_name= Ama-Wakamiya |pilgrimage= |annual_festival= |rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Wakamiya Shrine''' (若宮神社) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, an associated shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawashima Shrine|ja|川島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima Shrine|qq=|1=Kawashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766691}}, all located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 686</ref>
== Overview ==
Located within the precincts of Takihara-no-Miya in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17</ref> It stands on a raised area to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main shrine of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 86</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73</ref> The customary order of visiting shrines within Takihara-no-Miya precincts is Takihara-no-Miya, Takihara Namimiya, Wakamiya Shrine, Nagayuke Shrine, and Kawashima Shrine.<ref name="gk"/>
Wakamiya Shrine holds the highest Rank among the three subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It is also known as Ama-Wakamiya.<ref name="om6"/> The shrine faces south. According to the "Jingū Yōkō," the main hall is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a thatched roof, surrounded by a single-layered {{ill|mizugaki|ja|瑞垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mizugaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣|1=tamagaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3078312}} fence and gate, and features a {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687</ref>
The enshrined deity is Wakamiya no Kami.<ref name="uy"/><ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> It is considered a [[:en:Suijin|water god]] connected to Takihara.<ref name="gk"/> Another theory identifies the deity as {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|Mikumari no Kami=Mikumari no Kami|lt=Mikumari no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> The "Ōmiya Town History" states that the deity is unknown, but an annotation in the Jingū Administration Office's publication "O-Ise Mairi" refers to it as "Wakamiya no Kami."<ref name="om">Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 686–687</ref>
== History ==
[[File:Takihara-no-Miya 15.JPG|thumb|Shrine buildings before the relocation in November 2014. The left structure houses the sacred boat (御船倉), said to have been used by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>]]
The founding date is unknown.<ref name="om"/> The earliest known mention of Wakamiya Shrine is in the "Naikū Sengu-ki" from [[:en:Antei|Antei]] 2 ([[:en:1228|1228]]).<ref name="om6"/> A reference exists in the "Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" from [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1192|1192]]), noting visits to Namimiya, Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Ama-Wakamiya Shrines after the main rituals, but this was later annotated by Arakida Moritsune in [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] 5 ([[:en:1464|1464]]).<ref name="om"/>
Since [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 2 ([[:en:1662|1662]]), Wakamiya Shrine and Nagayuke Shrine have been included in the scope of {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} by the {{ill|Edo Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> From [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), they have been maintained by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="om7"/> Nagayuke Shrine was originally south-facing like Wakamiya Shrine but was reoriented westward in [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), with new enclosures and gates added.<ref name="om7"/> At that time, Kawashima Shrine was merged with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ise Bunka-sha (2008), ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Tour,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (1929), ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1,'' Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), ''History of Ōmiya Town: Historical Edition,'' Ōmiya Town, March 31, 1987, 1133 pages. * Gakken Publishing (2013), ''Let’s Go to Ise Grand Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011), ''Good Luck! Girls’♡ O-Ise Pilgrimage,'' Shufunotomo Co., Ltd., January 20, 2011, 95 pages. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

Wakamiya Shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:2014|2014]] (Heisei 26), with the previous relocation occurring in [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42).<ref name="uy"/>

* Approximately a 36-minute walk (about 1.9 km) from [[:en:Takihara Station|Takihara Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]].<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 83</ref> Limited train service, with gaps of 2–3 hours between trains, leading some travel guides to recommend using a car.<ref>Shufunotomo Co., Ltd. (2011): p. 44</ref> * Directly accessible from the "Takihara-no-Miya-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Matsusaka-Kumano Line|ja|松阪熊野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka-Kumano Line|qq=|1=Matsusaka-Kumano line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408350}} (formerly Nanki Express Bus) operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]].<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|language=ja|publisher=Mie Tourism Federation|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=Visit Mie|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref> * About 10 minutes (300 m) south from {{ill|Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|ja|大宮大台インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-Ōdai IC|qq=|1=Omiya-Odai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10934923}} on the [[:en:Kisei Expressway|Kisei Expressway]], via [[:en:Japan National Route 42|National Route 42 (Japan)]].<ref name="mkr"/> Parking available for 100 vehicles.<ref name="mkr"/>
